title: MuellerLiving HyperGrind Precision Electric Spice/Coffee Grinder Mill with Large Grinding Capacity and Powerful Motor also for Spices, Herbs, Nuts, Grains, Grey
description:
About this item
Consistent Grinds Every Time – Unlike many others on the market, the Mueller HyperGrind gives you perfect consistency & precision control over the coarseness of your Pour Over, Drip, Chemex, Cold Brew, French Press, Percolator,Turkish, Espresso, Herb, or Spices.
Compact & Stylish – Takes up minimum counter space and can be easily stored in cupboards and drawers but with its great modern Eurocentric styling, you just might want to leave it out in the open.
Quality Above All Else – Crafted with the same essence as traditional German precision machinery, our Mueller HyperGrind Precision Grinder is designed and tested through three professional grade inspections to outlast and outperform the competition!
Powerful & Efficient – Long lasting blades made from strong and durable stainless steel will easily grind any type of coffee you like, from coarse to fine quickly and efficiently with minimum grinding noise.
Superior Quality With Mueller – We only offer you the best quality products designed to make your life easier. Feel free to contact us. Our professional customer support is always here for you.
reviews_5s:
So I loved this grinder. Previous I’d gotten a grinder almost twice as expensive that looked cool but drove me crazy. It had a cup you had to remove that was sharp and it was loud and didn’t grind well. So when I got this grinder I was very happy with it. It doesn’t look like much but it’s a relatively quiet grinder that will turn your beans to dust in less then 30secs. I like a super fine grind for my pour over coffee and I hate spending a ton of time grinding my beans. So I was very pleased with this purchase.
There are a couple of cons: first the cord. It twists up and there’s nowhere to put it so you just have to deal with the spiraling of the cord. Every so often I’d try and untwist it with mediocre results. The 2nd con and prob what led to this grinder death, it’s kinda a btch to get the finely ground coffee out of the cup. If you use your fingers you get coffee grinds all up in your nails and it’s super hard to get out. If you like a coarser grind it shouldn’t be an issue. So bc I’m impatient and not wanting coffe grinds stuck under my nails all day, I’d bang on it when I was dumping it to try and get the coffee out. I now know there are little brushes you can get for that (but knowing me I’ll totally loose it). This just seems to be one of the overall cons for this type of grinder. There are pros and cons to the ones with removable cups too. One they’re pricer and I’ve had a couple that just didn’t seem to be as powerful. The upside of this grinder is the price, the speed and power. If they could make a retractable cord or a thing on the bottom where you could wrap extra cord up, it would make this grinder even better. I was actually going to reorder this grinder bc like I said the flaws are just the crap you deal with at this price point and this type of grinder; But I need a grinder ASAP and this one won’t come for a few days so I ordered a similar one with one same day shipping. I actually recommend this grinder, just watch the twisting cord and get one of those brushes if you do a fine grind. I think if you don’t smack it the way I did it’ll last a lot longer. Also the steel blades seem just as sharp as when I got them so def props for that.

Edit: so I bought a different grinder bc I needed it ASAP bc I can’t live without my am and afternoon coffee. While I like the looks and weight of the new grinder (I bought it bc it had same day shipping, not for looks) I’m even more impressed with Mueller grinder after using it. It takes twice as long and is messier. I will def be re-ordering the Mueller grinder again bc I miss it already. I’ll have to wait a couple of weeks bc I’m broke, but I will def be Re-ordering. I’ll also try and be more careful with it. No banging in on the counter and smacking the side to get the coffee out. Since the other grinder came with one of those brush thingies, I’ll do my best not to loose it and use it with my Mueller grinder. It’s amazing how this relatively cheap, light weight grinder has beat out every other grinder I’ve tried in the last five years. It’s def the power and speed. Watching the new grinder grind I could see how much slower the blades rotate. It takes twice as long and while grinding I have to tap the grinder to get the bigger pieces to mix with the finer pieces in order to get a uniform final grind. So I def rec the Murller grinder!I feed my ferrets 3 different foods, two of which are kibble, and need a grinder that will grind the kibble down to a powder for me to make their soupies out of. This grinder works great as long as I take its limitations into consideration and not work it to the point it overheats. I put a small amount of mixed kibble in it, pulse mix it a few times, then finish it into a powder by holding the button down for a few seconds. It is hard to find grinders that will powder your pets kibble without heating the food and spoiling it, so I was happy to find this grinder. Because I only do small batches and take precautionary measures to ensure it doesn't overheat, this grinder has performed well, is very easy to use, easy to wipe clean, and a great value and time saver for me.Compact so it doesn’t take up a lot of counter space and perfect for my morning grind. Good power and blends the bean nicely. Great price tooThis unit is surprisingly quiet for a grinder and gets the job done quickly. Unit feels very sturdy and well made, especially for this price point. Very impressed with this little unit.This grinder works exceptionally well. I’ve only used it a few times since purchasing but it it is very powerful and grinds spices in a jiffy. I am pleased with my purchase!!This works so well. I use it for either single keyrig or for French press. You can make the blend chunky or super fine in seconds.
Seems very durable.Love the ease of use and how quiet it is. Easy to clean and looks very sleek in white.I needed a grinder for nuts and seeds and this one is perfect for the job. I’ve had it for a couple of months but so far so good.I bought this to grind spices and grains. The included instructions say only use it for coffee, but so far it has worked well for grinding other small hard stuff. I have not used it all that much yet, but it does seem to work well and grinds quickly. It was also inexpensive (less than $10), which was a good value.

The only down side is the cup is not removeable, so that part is a little hard to clean. The plastic cover can be removed and washed though.Well made and quality! Necessary for my holiday baking season.
reviews_1s:
This thing is awful. I threw it away after about a month. I makes a big mess and I had no idea how it was happening. Every time I touched it or moved if, there was coffee grinds everywhere. Turns out, the base of the unit in the bottom was filling up with ground coffee. The coffee mess that I kept seeing everywhere, was coffee coming out the vents in the bottom of the grinder. The part with all of the electronics was PACKED with ground coffee. Completely unusable.This seemed a decent if uninspired coffee mill—worked fine, looks ok, does the job. But after a bit less than three years of ordinary service—two coffee drinkers in the house and we grind beans about once every two weeks—the mill just stopped working. I thought it needed a good cleaning, because sometimes a build-up of ground coffee can prevent the lid from fitting snugly, which in turn prevents the on button from making adequate contact. But after cleaning the mill still didn't work. Just gone dead. I then took it apart and tried to addict the contact point in the motor which initiates electricity flow, but that didn't work.

The trouble is that I'd like to have it fixed and not add plastic and other parts to the landfill, but so few repair places exist now and it would likely cost more to fix than to purchase a new one--from another manufacturer of course. This is party why we are a culture of waste.

I would discourage you from buying this coffee mill. I am going to buy the Krups equivalent, as we had one of those for a decade and it never died. We lost it when one box from a cross country move wandered off into the limbo land.Worked great. Bought Nov 2023. Until Jan 1, 2025. Then the tab pushing the mechanism broke. Brutal way to start the new year.
Our last grinder lasted over 5 years. New year, new me. No Mueller.The coffee grounds stick to everything when you open the lid and it is very messy. Something is wrong with the design of this grinder. I’ve never had a coffee grinder that was so messy. I’m not overfilling and I’m careful when opening the lid. It’s doesn’t matter and I lose half the grounds whenever I open the lid.2nd time purchasing. 1st one purchased in May 2024, died in Dec 2024. 2nd one purchased in Aug 2024, I just opened & it lasted 2 times & kaput.UPDATE:
Customer service was very quick to respond to my email complaint.
The response: Not covered under warranty. But offered 20% discount if I purchased again.

ALSO - Warranty for this product = One year.
Why would I want to replace this every one or two years? I purchased a Black and Decker to replace it (at less than the 20% discount for Mueller.)

_____
I purchased this since my Krups - that I had for 15 years - was just too noisy and a little inconsistent on the grind. I love this one! When it worked.

I cared for it exactly as mfr lists - no water, etc. Welp. Today, it just died. And I don't use it as much as most. I have maybe a cup a day and grind coffee every couple of months.

There is nothing listed here *OR* on their site about warranty for *any* of their products! Not good.Made in Germany! Way better than that stuff made in China, right! NOT!! I read a few reviews that mentioned this same defect, but I let the "Germany" shtick sway my judgement. OK, so the shaft on which the blade is mounted comes up through the bottom of the bowl OFF CENTER. I'm glad I plugged it in and turned it on before pouring in some of my expensive beans that would then have had metal shavings in them. The noise when I first turned it on was frightening, as the blade started cutting a groove into the side of the stainless bowl. What a disappointment this thing was. Also, don't let the advertising about how much better a job it does grinding beans than the other similar coffee grinders. NOT! One look into the top of this thing shows that other than the cutter being off-center, it looks EXACTLY like every other coffee grinder out there. The blade and even the plastic nut that holds it down look exactly like the 10-year-old Mr. Coffee grinder I gave to my neighbor in anticipation of having a nice new one. Also, none of these blade "grinders" actually grind anything. They chop. Like food processors, they chop, not grind. They only use the word "grinder" because it is more acceptable than saying "coffee chopper." Bottom line, believe the other reviews: this thing is a poorly-assembled, substandard version of the everyday, ordinary cheap coffee "grinders" out there. Buy one made in China; at least they have decent quality control.The one I received didn't work. It was bigger than the brand I used to have.Grinds a smaller amount than expected. Difficult to clean if not impossible. Messy to use. Sorely disappointed. We have had two Braun grainders-one for spices and one for coffee. We thought the blades were not as sharp as they should be so we purchased this one. Fortunately, we didn't take it to the thrift store and will now use it for spices and use the "spice one" as our coffee grinder.
It is quiet but we have discovered that quiet isn't that important. We won't clean it out to take it to the thrift store. I really wanted this coffee grinder to work well. The off and on button is not what is pictured in the "happy" videos but that wouldn't have been a deal breaker.Our Krups grinder went to that great cafe in the sky in February, and I foolishly clicked on this item because it was at the top of the search results and rEcOmMeNdeD by our corporate overlords. Here's the lowlights: 1) It grinds unevenly. Unless you like a half-dozen whole beans in your espresso grind, then this is the product for you! 2) Instantly jams with grinder dust and grounds due to the design. There's no way to avoid it. 3) Good luck trying to pour the grounds from the grinder to your ground coffee storage. You're going to have grounds and dust - and the occasional unground bean because that's how poorly designed this grinder is - all over your counter.
